name: Ubiquiti airMAX PowerBeam 5AC
title: Ubiquiti airMAX PowerBeam 5AC
short_description: 5GHz, 25 dBi 2x2 Dual antenna, 1x GLAN, POE-RJ-45, 802.11ac, Throughput 450+ Mbps, Range 15+ km.
description: The airMAX PowerBeam 5AC Gen2, bearing the model manufacture part number PBE-5AC-Gen2, is a cutting-edge wireless network bridge developed by Ubiquiti Networks. This device is engineered to deliver exceptional point-to-point (PtP) and point-to-multipoint (PtMP) connectivity, making it an integral component of modern high-speed wireless networks. Operating within the 5 GHz frequency band, the PowerBeam 5AC Gen2 utilizes Ubiquiti's proprietary airMAX ac technology to provide superior throughput, network stability, and scalability.

detailed_description.heading: High-Performance 5 GHz PtP Bridge
detailed_description.text: The airMAX PowerBeam 5AC Gen2, bearing the model manufacture part number PBE-5AC-Gen2, is a cutting-edge wireless network bridge developed by Ubiquiti Networks. This device is engineered to deliver exceptional point-to-point (PtP) and point-to-multipoint (PtMP) connectivity, making it an integral component of modern high-speed wireless networks. Operating within the 5 GHz frequency band, the PowerBeam 5AC Gen2 utilizes Ubiquiti's proprietary airMAX ac technology to provide superior throughput, network stability, and scalability.

detailed_description.heading: Compatibility and Use Cases
detailed_description.text: The PowerBeam 5AC Gen2 is fully compatible with other airMAX ac devices. This includes a wide variety of Ubiquiti's outdoor wireless access points, bridges, and customer premises equipment (CPE) that also utilize airMAX ac technology. Such compatibility ensures efficient point-to-point (PtP) and point-to-multipoint (PtMP) connections, enabling network operators to design flexible, high-capacity wireless networks that can be easily scaled or modified to meet evolving needs. Also, keep in mind that the PowerBeam 5AC can coexist in networks that also incorporate UniFi devices, especially when using separate network segments. However, it's important to note that the PowerBeam operates using airOS, and UniFi devices are managed through the UniFi Controller. Direct interaction is limited due to the different technologies and purposes these series serve, with UniFi focusing on managed indoor and semi-outdoor Wi-Fi networking and airMAX targeting long-distance outdoor wireless connectivity.

detailed_description.heading: Design and Antenna
detailed_description.text: At the heart of the PowerBeam 5AC Gen2's design is its 25 dBi gain, directional antenna, integrated into a compact and aerodynamic form factor. This integration enhances the device's ability to focus the radio signal, significantly increasing range and reducing interference. The antenna's precision and directivity are critical for establishing 15+ km wireless links, ensuring that data transmission remains robust and reliable over kilometers, even in challenging environmental conditions.

detailed_description.heading: airMAX ac Technology
detailed_description.text: Ubiquiti's airMAX ac technology underpins the PowerBeam 5AC Gen2's performance, incorporating a custom silicon chip that supports an advanced modulation scheme. This technology enables the device to achieve higher throughput rates compared to earlier standards, making it well-suited for bandwidth-intensive applications such as HD video streaming, VoIP communications, and large data transfers. The implementation of Time Division Multiple Access (TDMA) protocol within airMAX ac technology also reduces network latency and increases time efficiency by organizing data transmission, allowing for more concurrent data sessions and improved network performance.

detailed_description.heading: Innovative Features and Installation
detailed_description.text: One of the standout features of the PowerBeam 5AC Gen2 is its innovative design aimed at maximizing ease of installation and flexibility in deployment. The device includes a proprietary mounting bracket, designed to offer both stability and adjustability, facilitating precise alignment and optimal signal reception. This mounting system, combined with the PowerBeam 5AC Gen2's all-weather enclosure, ensures that the device can be deployed in a wide range of outdoor environments, from rural settings to urban rooftops, without compromising on performance or durability. Additionally, Ubiquiti's InnerFeed® technology integrates the radio into the feedhorn of an antenna, so there is no need for cables to connect the radio to the antenna. This improves performance because it eliminates cable losses. The PowerBeam 5AC requires no tools for assembly; only a single wrench is required for pole-mounting.
